title: Add support for overlay volume mounts in podman.

comments.body: :umbrella: The latest upstream changes (presumably #3425) made this pull request unmergeable. Please resolve the merge conflicts.
Hope to get back to this soon.
:umbrella: The latest upstream changes (presumably #3728) made this pull request unmergeable. Please resolve the merge conflicts.
@rhatdan looks like you've merge conflicts here.
Ping (to move it up the mails)
@rhatdan is this worth pursuing?
Yes, I need to get back to it.  Or someone else can grab it and run with it, if they have time.
@cevich @giuseppe @vrothberg @baude @mheon These tests are failing with permission denied on an overlay volume mount.  What is special_testing versus regular testing?

> What is special_testing versus regular testing?

Those are integration tests for items that didn't fit in the "testing_task" matrix.  So rootless, podman-in-podman, etc.  Feel free to add one if it makes sense (and you want to run integration tests).  You'll find them all referenced in `contrib/cirrus/integration_test.sh`

Also FWIW / reminder: There is also a second partition ready-to-go on all VMs that have a non-empty `/root/second_partition_ready` file (device name inside).  For example if you want to add tests running on device-mapper or other fancy storage setups :smile:

:umbrella: The latest upstream changes (presumably #4980) made this pull request unmergeable. Please resolve the merge conflicts.
Went to check which version was the first with this feature and realized it's only in buildah, not podman... what help is needed to get this feature merged?
Just need to get time to work on it.  If you want to grab it and drive it over the finish line, your help would be appreciated.  I am concentrating on the `containers.conf` patches right now.
still fighting with the CI.  LGTM overall, but there are still a few unanswered questions/points from @mheon that I'd like to see his opinion on.

We should not merge until the config rewrite issue has been resolved. Modifying c.config.Spec is never safe outside of `podman system migrate`. We should move this into `generateSpec()` and not modify the OCI spec in c.config, but the final version we're going to write to disk. Unfortunately, this happens after the container is mounted, which may impact our ability to determine what to mount; we'd have to separate the mounting and spec modification logic.

(I'd really prefer it if we stored these separately in the database and did not introduce non-standard semantics into our handling of the spec, but there is some precedent with SELinux `:z` and `:Z` flags)
A friendly reminder that this PR had no activity for 30 days.
Friendly ping to revive the PR
Yes plan on giving this PR to an Intern
